steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined sentence is: "Such analysis, in turn, can contribute to reports of exaggerated effects." The phrase in turn signals a chain of consequences. The preceding sentence states that small sample sizes increase the potential for biased analysis. The underlined sentence names what biased analysis can then lead to. The question asks what function this sentence serves in the passage.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It summarizes a shift in scientists' understanding of how *Pomacentrus amboinensis* has responded to ocean acidification.’: the sentence says nothing about a shift in understanding of how the fish responded to ocean acidification. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It emphasizes the magnitude of the effect reported by Price and colleagues of ocean acidification on *Pomacentrus amboinensis*.’: the sentence is about what biased analysis produces, not about restating Price et al.'s original findings. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It counters the objection of Morris and colleagues to studies that rely on relatively small sample sizes.’: the sentence actually extends Morris et al.'s point, not opposes it. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It elaborates on a potential consequence of Price and colleagues' reliance on a relatively small sample size.’: Morris et al. warned that small samples increase biased analysis; the underlined sentence adds that such analysis can then lead to exaggerated reports — a downstream consequence of the small sample size. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
